title: Add Town Office Staff Dashboard for Viewing Problem Reports

requirements.text: Create a new `TownOfficeDashboardPage` at `frontend/src/pages/TownOfficeDashboardPage.tsx` that is accessible only to authenticated admin/staff users (following the same `useIsAdmin` pattern used in `ReportProblemPage.tsx` and `EventsPage.tsx`). The page should display all submitted problem reports fetched via the existing `useGetProblemReports` React Query hook. Each report card must show: problem type, location description, detailed description, reporter name, reporter contact info (email/phone), and submission date. Include a delete button on each card for admins. If the user is not authenticated as admin, show a message indicating that access is restricted to town office staff. Style the page consistently with the existing Northern Maine wilderness theme (forest greens, slate grays, amber accents) using the same card/section layout patterns found in `ServicesPage.tsx` and `ReportProblemPage.tsx`. Include a prominent page heading such as 'Town Office — Problem Reports Dashboard'.

quotes: make a page on the site for the town office members can see what people have reorted

acceptanceCriteria: The page fetches and displays all submitted problem reports using the existing `useGetProblemReports` hook.
Each report card shows problem type, location, description, reporter name, reporter contact info, and submission date.
Admin users can delete individual reports from this page.
Non-admin or unauthenticated users see a restricted-access message instead of the reports.
The page is styled consistently with the existing Northern Maine wilderness theme.

requirements.text: Register the `TownOfficeDashboardPage` as a route in `frontend/src/App.tsx` at path `/town-office/reports`, following the same route definition pattern used for existing pages.

quotes: make a page on the site for the town office members can see what people have reorted

acceptanceCriteria: The route is registered in `frontend/src/App.tsx` using the same pattern as other existing routes.

requirements.text: Add a 'Town Office Dashboard' link to both the desktop and mobile menus in `frontend/src/components/Navigation.tsx`, and add it to the quick links in `frontend/src/components/Footer.tsx`. The link should point to `/town-office/reports` and be labeled 'Town Office Dashboard' (or 'Staff Dashboard'). Follow the same link patterns used for existing pages. Optionally, only display this link when the user is authenticated as an admin.

quotes: make a page on the site for the town office members can see what people have reorted

acceptanceCriteria: A 'Town Office Dashboard' (or 'Staff Dashboard') link appears in both the desktop and mobile navigation menus.
A corresponding quick link is added to the Footer.
Link patterns are consistent with existing navigation items.

constraints: Reuse the existing `useGetProblemReports` and `useDeleteProblemReport` React Query hooks — do not create new backend endpoints.
Access control must follow the same `useIsAdmin` pattern used in `ReportProblemPage.tsx` and `EventsPage.tsx`.
Do not modify any files listed under frontend.immutablePaths.
nonGoals: Creating new backend data models or API endpoints for this feature.
